aircraft maintenance & systems integration testing (functional & system), efb (flight operations), elogbook, flight management system (fms) and surveillance & navigation activities, looking for an opportunity to excel in the field of avionics. actively interact with customer to plan, coordinate and work package execution and managing the team. avionics system expertise: a350 fsang electronic flight bag (efb) a320 neo - flight data interface management unit (fdimu) a350/a380/a330 neo - aircraft condition monitoring systems (acms) a350 flight management system (fms) a320 neo - basics of flight warning system (fws / fwc) flight test installation (real time lab/flight data recording instrument for testing purpose) current organization: company: safran engineering services, bangalore, india designation: engineer ii (old: system engineer) duration: 4.7 years (from 24.06.2013 to till date) project profile: system integration & installation (v&v) programme: airbus a320/a330 neo, a350 xwb and a380 system scope: aircraft maintenance systems (health monitoring): flight data interface management unit (fdimu) aircraft condition monitoring system (acms) centralized fault display interface unit (cfdiu) team: systems engineering - system integration & installation (v&v) nature of job: system integration testing (v&v) lab/ground/flight test request - review test scenario / procedure creation to test the system under test on test bench environment (environment: lab test benches, flight simulator or system integration benches (sib) and functional integration benches (fib)) execution of created test scenarios by counterpart team at toulouse lab/ground/flight tests recorded data extraction (protocol: arinc 429) and analysis of the system behavior using the extracted data and observations during the test campaign. raising problem report (joint technical event) of any deviations in the system behavior encountered during the test campaign. consolidated test analysis report creation on the tested product of the test campaign. project profile: flight operation programme: airbus a350 system scope: electronic flight bag (efb) flight management system (fms) jeppesen (enroute & terminal) charts team: flight operations support onsite experience: undergone 3 months of training in efb flight operations support, located at airbus saint martin, toulouse, france. nature of job: flight operations - performance analysis (high & low level) and estimate the fms data status. creating scenario of the particular aircraft (e.g.: a350) creation of eff mission load efb validation (based on scenario) this job is mainly performed on lpc-ng to validate the interfaces between the following software from an operational point of view: fms page, electronic flight folder (eff), loadbox, exportbox, charts (lido & jeppesen), take-off performance application (topa), landing performance application (ldpa), in-flight performance application (ifpa), loadsheet (wba), and ops library. validation of lpc-ng versions (e.g.: l 5.1.1, l5.1.3) validation of elearning i-pad project profile: surveillance and radio navigation systems programme: sa/lr system scope: radio altimeter automatic direction finder distance measuring equipment team: systems engineering surveillance and radio navigation systems (v&v) nature of job: problem investigation lab/ground/flight test request - review test scenario / procedure creation to test the system under test on test bench environment execution of created test scenarios by counterpart team at toulouse lab/ground/flight tests recorded data extraction (protocol: arinc 429) and analysis of the system behavior using the extracted data and observations during the test campaign. raising problem report (joint technical event) of any deviations in the system behavior encountered during the test campaign. consolidated test analysis report creation on the tested product of the test campaign. try to find out the root-cause of the identifying problem. worked on honeywell sara project for radio altimeter investigation project profile: maintenance systems programme: sa/lr and a350 system scope: elogbook centralized maintenance system team: systems engineering maintenance systems (v&v) nature of job: update the requirements as per maintenance & pilot team. and end-to-end execution of the product development life cycle requirement development, detailed design, review and testing. elogbook activities: hlpts issue 2.3 in doors. (requirements update) sid-proof reading board/ground hlpts batching hlpts final export document-requirements update sdd c2.0 status update capitalization of elb specification in doors activity cms activities: tra s2c & s2c jte defect analysis vvos status update in doors s2c vvo doors status update following mca status update srd doors status update after vvo update. academic qualifications: (in the order of highest degree) postgraduate: m.e. avionics, 2013 passed out, cgpa : 8.6 out of 10 madras institute of technology, university departments, anna university - chennai undergraduate: b.e. electronics and communication engineering, 2011 passed out, 81% velammal college of engineering and technology, anna university - tirunelveli higher secondary level: muslim higher secondary school, abiramam. 2007 passed out, 82.50% tamil nadu state board secondary level: muslim higher secondary school, abiramam. 2005 passed out, 86.40% tamil nadu state board application exposure: programming languages: python, vba excel macros design tools: gnat programming studio (gps), matlab-simulink, v&v tools: airbus tools such as atole, bde, gmri, visage plot, ltm, grefie. guidelines & standards: do-178b, arinc-429, do-160e, arp4754a, arp4761, do254 traceability tools: ibm rational doors problem repository tool: ltm, track dev achievements and activities: published a paper in international conference detection and identification of air vehicles based on its acoustic signature using matlab at jntu hyderabad. presented a paper on direction of arrival measurement of air vehicles based on its acoustic signature using matlab at drdo international conference. this is an aeronautical development establishment defense project.
